definitions.text: To remove or block an opening, gap or passage through. To move a thing, or part of a thing, nearer to another so that the gap or opening between the two is removed.
examples: Close the door behind you when you leave.

definitions.text: To move a thing, or part of a thing, nearer to another so that the gap or opening between the two is removed.
examples: Close the door behind you when you leave.
They closed the road for the festival.
Ice has closed the channel to shipping.
We owe them our thanks for bringing the project to a successful close.

definitions.text: The manner of shutting; the union of parts; junction.
The point at the end of a sales pitch when the consumer is asked to buy.
Having little difference or distance in place, position, or abstractly; see also close to. At little distance; near in space or time.
At little distance; near in space or time.
Almost, but not quite (getting to an answer, goal, or other state); near.
examples: We were so close to winning!

definitions.text: In a close manner (limited contexts; more often closely). So as to leave or create little distance or space between objects.
examples: The car behind was following too close and could not brake in time.

definitions.text: So as to leave or create little distance or space between objects.
examples: The car behind was following too close and could not brake in time.

definitions.text: An enclosed field, especially a field enclosed around a (usually religious) building.
A street that ends in a dead end.
A very narrow alley between two buildings, often overhung by one of the buildings above the ground floor.
